response_text: {"session_id":"run-114","lens":"scout","relevant_files":[{"path":"src/forge/mod.rs","line_range":"1-120","role":"defines current Forge/Provider trait abstraction, likely GitHub-only today"},{"path":"src/forge/github.rs","line_range":"1-300","role":"existing GitHub implementation using octocrab, reference pattern for new forges"},{"path":"src/scanner/mod.rs","line_range":"1-200","role":"SecurityScanner core, consumes Forge trait to fetch repos/PRs/commits for scanning"},{"path":"src/scanner/pattern_match.rs","line_range":"1-150","role":"pattern matching engine (regex/tree-sitter based) invoked per-file, forge-agnostic but depends on file fetch abstraction"},{"path":"src/config.rs","line_range":"1-180","role":"config parsing for forge credentials/endpoints, needs extension for multi-forge auth"},{"path":"Cargo.toml","line_range":"1-60","role":"dependency manifest, currently lists octocrab; needs gitlab/gitea/azure clients or generic http client"},{"path":"tests/integration/github_scan.rs","line_range":"1-100","role":"integration test pattern for GitHub scanning, template for new forge tests"},{"path":"CONTRIBUTING.md","line_range":"1-80","role":"documents conventional commits, DCO sign-off, test requirements"}],"conventions":{"commits":"Conventional Commits enforced (feat/fix/chore scopes), DCO sign-off required on all commits per CONTRIBUTING.md","testing":"unit tests colocated with modules using #[cfg(test)] mod tests, integration tests under tests/ directory per forge/feature, mock HTTP servers (wiremock or mockito) used to simulate forge APIs","linting":"cargo fmt and cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -D warnings enforced in CI","error_handling":"thiserror-based custom error enums per module (e.g., ForgeError), anyhow used at binary/CLI boundary, Result<T, E> propagated with ? operator, no unwrap/panic in library code"},"patterns":["Trait-based abstraction (Forge trait) implemented per provider, dependency-injected into SecurityScanner","Config-driven credential resolution (env var fallback then config file) per forge","Builder pattern for constructing forge clients with base_url override for self-hosted instances (relevant for Gitea/Forgejo/GitLab self-managed)","Async trait methods via async-trait crate for forge API calls","Feature flags in Cargo.toml gating optional forge integrations to control binary size"],"related_issues":[{"number":735,"title":"Abstract GitHub-specific scanning logic behind a Forge trait","relevance":"introduced the initial Forge trait refactor that #1205 builds directly on top of"},{"number":736,"title":"PR: Implement Forge trait and migrate GitHubForge to use it","relevance":"merged PR establishing the trait signature, async methods, and error type that new forges (GitLab, Gitea/Forgejo, Azure DevOps) must implement"}],"constraints":["New forge implementations must conform to existing async Forge trait signature to avoid breaking SecurityScanner call sites","Must support self-hosted/on-prem instances (custom base_url) for GitLab, Gitea/Forgejo, and Azure DevOps, not just SaaS endpoints","Authentication schemes differ significantly (PAT for GitLab/Gitea, OAuth/PAT for Azure DevOps) requiring extensible credential config without breaking existing GitHub config schema","CI must remain fast; adding heavy per-forge SDK dependencies risks increasing compile times and binary size, suggesting feature flags or a shared minimal HTTP client","Backward compatibility: existing GitHub-only configs and CLI flags must continue to work unchanged"],"test_coverage":"Existing coverage is GitHub-only: unit tests validate GitHubForge request construction and response parsing; integration tests under tests/integration/github_scan.rs use mocked HTTP responses to validate SecurityScanner end-to-end flow against GitHub. No test scaffolding currently exists for GitLab, Gitea/Forgejo, or Azure DevOps; new forges will need equivalent mock-based integration tests and trait conformance unit tests.","library_findings":[{"library":"octocrab","version":"0.x (pinned in Cargo.toml, likely ^0.38-0.41 range)","relevant_api":"Octocrab client builder, repos().list_commits(), pulls().list() used in GitHubForge","notes":"GitHub-specific; not reusable for other forges, confirms need for separate per-forge clients rather than a shared SDK"},{"library":"gitlab (rust crate, aka gitlab_api or gitlab-rs)","version":"not yet a dependency; would need to add ~0.16+","notes":"Provides synchronous client by default; async support via feature flag or reqwest wrapper needed to match existing async Forge trait; API surface differs from octocrab significantly, requiring adapter layer"},{"library":"reqwest + serde_json (generic HTTP approach)","version":"reqwest already likely a transitive dep via octocrab","notes":"Gitea/Forgejo and Azure DevOps lack mature idiomatic Rust SDKs; a thin generic REST client built on reqwest with per-forge endpoint/auth adapters is the more consistent path across all three new forges, avoiding three divergent third-party crate APIs"}],"approaches":[{"name":"Per-forge SDK adapters implementing existing Forge trait","description":"Add gitlab crate for GitLab, and hand-rolled reqwest-based clients for Gitea/Forgejo and Azure DevOps (no mature SDKs exist), each wrapped in a struct implementing the async Forge trait from PR #736. Extend config.rs to parse forge-specific auth blocks.","pros":["Reuses proven trait abstraction from #735/#736 with minimal SecurityScanner changes","Leverages mature, community-maintained gitlab crate where available reducing maintenance burden","Incremental: forges can be added in separate PRs behind feature flags"],"cons":["Inconsistent implementation style across forges (SDK vs hand-rolled) increases review complexity","gitlab crate's sync-first design may require async wrapper shims, adding friction","Three new sets of integration tests and mocks needed"],"complexity":"medium","files_touched":9},{"name":"Unified generic REST client with per-forge endpoint mapping","description":"Build a single generic ForgeHttpClient on reqwest handling auth header injection, pagination, and rate-limit backoff, with each forge providing only a thin config (base_url templates, auth scheme, JSON path mappings) rather than a full bespoke implementation. GitHub could optionally migrate to this too for consistency, though out of scope initially.","pros":["Most elegant and DRY: single code path to test, debug, and extend for future forges (e.g., Bitbucket later)","Easier to add self-hosted base_url support uniformly across all forges","Reduces dependency surface (drops need for gitlab crate)"],"cons":["Larger upfront refactor touching config.rs, error types, and possibly GitHubForge for consistency","Risk of over-abstracting before real usage patterns from 3 forges are known","Higher initial complexity and review burden, slower to ship first forge"],"complexity":"complex","files_touched":14},{"name":"Feature-flagged incremental rollout, GitLab first","description":"Ship only GitLab support behind a 'gitlab' cargo feature in this issue using the gitlab crate, following the exact pattern from PR #736, and defer Gitea/Forgejo and Azure DevOps to follow-up issues once the pattern is validated in production.","pros":["Fastest to ship, smallest reviewable diff","Validates trait abstraction against a second real-world forge before committing to a broader design","Keeps binary size and compile times in check via feature flags"],"cons":["Does not fully close issue #1205 which explicitly scopes all three forges","Defers harder problems (Gitea/Forgejo and Azure DevOps lacking SDKs) rather than solving them","May require re-litigating design if generic client approach is later deemed better"],"complexity":"simple","files_touched":5}],"recommendation":"Recommend approach 1 (per-forge SDK adapters implementing the existing Forge trait) as the pragmatic middle ground: it directly extends the abstraction already validated in issue #735 / PR #736, closes #1205 in full scope across all three forges, and isolates risk per-forge via feature flags. Approach 2 (unified generic REST client) is the more elegant long-term direction and should be proposed as a follow-up refactor once GitLab, Gitea/Forgejo, and Azure DevOps implementations reveal common patterns worth consolidating -- attempting that generic abstraction now, before three real implementations exist, risks premature and incorrect generalization."}
